name: humanity (whom he favors)
shortDescription: Humanity, in Babylonian mythology, is a creation favored by the god Ea (Enki). They are central to the purpose of the world as understood by the Babylonians.
description: In Babylonian mythology, humanity holds a unique position as the creation favored by the god Ea (Enki), also known as Nudimmud. The creation of humankind is primarily detailed in the Atrahasis epic, where the gods, weary of their own labor, sought a solution to alleviate their burden. Initially, minor gods (the Igigi) rebelled against the more powerful Anunnaki, forcing them to find a solution. Ea, with the help of the mother goddess Mami (also known as Nintu or Belet-ili), devised a plan to create humans from clay, mixed with the blood of a slain god, Kingu. This divine essence imbued humanity with intelligence and the capacity for labor, effectively taking over the workload of the gods. Humans were created to serve the gods, offering sacrifices and maintaining the temples. However, humanity's rapid growth and noisy existence eventually disturbed the gods, leading to attempts to control their population through plagues, famine, and ultimately, the great flood. Ea, however, warned Atrahasis, a wise and pious man, to build an ark and save himself and his family, thus ensuring the survival of humanity. The relationship between Ea and humanity is one of protector and benefactor, as he repeatedly intervenes on their behalf despite the gods' frustrations with them. Humanity's purpose, therefore, is intertwined with service to the gods and maintaining cosmic order.
